source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A beam of electrons is scattered by a fixed scattering target as shown in Fig. 3.27. The electrons are elastically scattered. Each electron has an energy $E=\frac{5}{3} m_{0} c^{2}$ and the beam has a flux of $Q$ electrons per second. What are the magnitude and direction of the force on the scattering target due to the electrons?
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text: $\frac{4 \sqrt{2}}{3} Q m_{0} c$, acting vertically onto the target
extraction_notes: image field contains a PIL object representation rather than a file path or URL

Force direction on the target depends on the scattering directions/angles, which are visual-spatial details not fully specified in text.
multi_step: Solving requires combining relativistic energy with momentum/elastic-scattering reasoning, so it is not a single direct lookup.
The requested output has two parts (magnitude and direction), implying vector reasoning from the figure plus calculation.
The text gives beam flux `Q` and particle energy, suggesting per-electron momentum change must be converted into total force.
verifiability: The answer is specific and constrained: a closed-form magnitude plus a direction, which is checkable against physics derivation.
Because the geometry comes from the image, verification is possible but depends on correctly reading the figure, so it is high but not maximal.

candidate_problem_record.subject: 物理
candidate_problem_record.raw_question_text: A beam of electrons is scattered by a fixed scattering target as shown in Fig. 3.27. The electrons are elastically scattered. Each electron has an energy $E=\frac{5}{3} m_{0} c^{2}$ and the beam has a flux of $Q$ electrons per second. What are the magnitude and direction of the force on the scattering target due to the electrons?
candidate_problem_record.raw_answer_text: $\frac{4 \sqrt{2}}{3} Q m_{0} c$, acting vertically onto the target
candidate_problem_record.multi_solution_policy_rationale: 该数据集保留多解潜力评估，但不默认强推多解 agent。

extraction_notes: image field contains a PIL object representation rather than a file path or URL
metadata.reasoning_chain: Since the electrons are elastically scattered, they have the same speed before and after scattering and conservation of the momentum parallel to the target requires that the incident and scattering angles are equal. Then after scattering the normal component of the momentum changes sign but remains the same in magnitude. Hence $\Delta p=2 p_{n}=2 m_{0}\,\gamma v \cos 45^{\circ}=\frac{4 \sqrt{2}}{3} m_{0} c$. The force $F$ on the target due to the beam of electrons is equal to the impulse given to it per unit time by the beam. As $Q$ electrons impinge on the target in unit time, $F=2 p_{n} Q=\frac{4 \sqrt{2}}{3} Q m_{0} c$ and it acts vertically onto the target.
